introduction:
The Ration Card in Karnataka is issued by the Department of Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs, Government of Karnataka. It entitles eligible households to receive subsidised food commodities — rice, wheat, ragi, sugar, and kerosene — through the Public Distribution System (PDS) at authorised Fair Price Shops (Raitha Samparka Kendras).

The ration card is also a widely accepted identity and address proof across Karnataka. The state runs flagship welfare schemes like Anna Bhagya (10 kg free rice per person/month for BPL families) and Indira Canteen that are linked to your ration card status.
quickFacts:
Important to Know
  • Applying for a new ration card is FREE of cost
  • Processing time is approximately 30 working days
  • You must be a permanent resident of Karnataka to apply
  • Only one ration card per family is allowed
  • Aadhaar linking is mandatory for all family members
  • BPL families get Anna Bhagya benefit: 10 kg free rice per person/month
  • Track your application at ahara.kar.nic.in
  • Helpline: 1800-425-9339 (Toll-Free)
documentsRequired:
Documents Required for Ration Card

Note: Submit self-attested photocopies at the Nadakacheri / Seva Sindhu center and carry original documents for verification.
Identity Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Voter ID Card
  • PAN Card
  • Passport
  • Driving Licence
  • MGNREGS Job Card
Address Proof (Any one)
  • Aadhaar Card
  • Electricity Bill (recent)
  • Water / Telephone Bill
  • Bank Passbook
  • Registered Rent Agreement
  • Property Tax Receipt
Income & Family Documents
  • Income Certificate (from Tahsildar / Nadakacheri)
  • Birth Certificates of children
  • Marriage Certificate (if applicable)
  • Passport-size photos of all family members
  • Caste Certificate (if applying for BPL/AAY)
Category Guide
  • AAY (Antyodaya): Poorest of poor — free grain entitlement
  • PHH (Priority Households): BPL families under NFSA
  • NPHH (Non-Priority): APL / above poverty line families

Note: PHH / AAY eligibility requires income certificate from Tahsildar.
onlineApplication:
Method 1: Apply Online via Seva Sindhu / Ahara Portal

Karnataka offers online ration card applications through the Seva Sindhu citizen services portal and the Ahara (Food Department) portal. You need an Aadhaar-linked mobile number.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit the Official Portal
    Go to sevasindhu.karnataka.gov.in or ahara.kar.nic.in
  2. Register / Login
    Click "New User Registration". Enter your Aadhaar number, mobile number, and verify with the OTP received on your mobile.
  3. Select Service
    After login, navigate to Food & Civil Supplies \u2192 "New Ration Card Application" (Service Code: FS-01)
  4. Fill Application Form
    Provide all required details accurately:
    • Head of family name (as per Aadhaar)
    • All family members with Aadhaar numbers
    • Select ration card category (PHH / NPHH / AAY)
    • Select your Taluk, Hobli, Village and nearest FPS
  5. Upload Documents
    Upload scanned copies of identity proof, address proof, income certificate, and family photos. File size limit: 1 MB per file (JPG/PDF)
  6. Submit & Note Acknowledgement Number
    Click Submit. An Acknowledgement Number is generated. Save this number! SMS will be sent to your mobile.
  7. Verification by Food Inspector
    A Food Inspector will visit your home within 15 working days to verify details and documents.
Tip: Keep all original documents ready at home during the Food Inspector's visit.
offlineApplication:
Method 2: Apply Offline at Nadakacheri / Taluk Office (Direct Visit)

You can directly visit your nearest Nadakacheri (Atalji Janasnehi Kendra) or the local Taluk Office to apply without internet access.

Step-by-Step Process:
  1. Visit Nadakacheri / Taluk Office
    Working hours: Monday \u2013 Saturday, 10:00 AM \u2013 5:30 PM (closed on 2nd Saturday & public holidays)
  2. Collect Application Form
    Obtain the Ration Card application form from the Nadakacheri counter. The form is provided free of cost.
  3. Fill the Form Carefully
    Fill in Kannada or English, in black ink / BLOCK LETTERS:
    • Head of family name (as per Aadhaar)
    • All family member details & Aadhaar numbers
    • Taluk, Hobli, Village / Ward details
    • Category required (PHH / NPHH / AAY)
    • Active mobile number
  4. Submit Documents & Get Receipt
    Submit form + self-attested photocopies of all required documents. Carry originals for counter verification. You receive an Acknowledgement Receipt with application number.
  5. Home Verification by Food Inspector
    A Food Inspector visits your home within 15 working days to verify details.
  6. Collect Ration Card
    After approval, collect your ration card from the Nadakacheri / Taluk Office within 30 working days. An SMS notification is sent to your mobile.
Processing Time: Approximately 30 working days from date of application submission.
afterApplying:
After Applying \u2014 What Next?

Track Your Application

Visit ahara.kar.nic.in or sevasindhu.karnataka.gov.in
Enter your Acknowledgement Number to check current status.

Download Digital Ration Card

Once approved, download your ration card from the Ahara or Seva Sindhu portal. The digital copy is valid at all Fair Price Shops (Raitha Samparka Kendras).

Anna Bhagya Scheme

After getting your BPL/PHH ration card, register for the Anna Bhagya scheme at your nearest FPS to receive 10 kg free rice per family member per month.

Raise a Grievance

If not processed within 30 days, call Toll-Free: 1800-425-9339 or visit ahara.kar.nic.in to file an online complaint.
